pattern: Each position's jump distance just becomes a modular offset into the same array, so wrap the index arithmetically instead of physically walking it step by step.
hints: Walking one step at a time in a circular array is unnecessary -- landing after d steps from index i is the same as jumping straight to (i + d) mod n.
Handle negative offsets by adding n before taking the modulo, so the computed index always stays non-negative.
A value of zero never moves at all -- it copies straight into the result unchanged.

edge_inputs.description: single element, always zero-length jump onto itself
single positive element wraps fully around back to itself
jump distance exactly equal to array length wraps to the same index
negative jump larger in magnitude than the array length
extreme positive and negative values at the constraint limits
mixed positive and negative jumps landing on each other

stress.description: arrays across the full length and value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(100, size if size >= 1 else 1))
    nums = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
